crisis.title: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
crisis.description: Call or text 988 anytime. Free, confidential support 24/7.

crisis.title: Crisis Text Line
crisis.description: Text HOME to 741741 to reach a trained crisis counselor.

crisis.title: NAMI Helpline
crisis.description: National Alliance on Mental Illness. Call 1-800-950-6264.

crisis.title: Anxiety & Depression Association
crisis.description: Find a therapist, support groups, and crisis resources.

articles.title: How Panic Attacks Work
articles.description: A calm, science-based look at what's actually happening in your body during a panic attack.

articles.title: The Science of Breathing
articles.description: How controlling your breath directly calms your nervous system. From Stanford neuroscientist Andrew Huberman.

articles.title: What Is the Vagus Nerve?
articles.description: The key nerve connecting your brain and body — and why stimulating it helps anxiety.

articles.title: Grounding Techniques That Actually Work
articles.description: A practical overview of sensory grounding from the Anxiety & Depression Association of America.

articles.title: Why Your Body Goes Into Panic Mode
articles.description: The fight-or-flight response explained simply and without alarm.

articles.title: The Odd Comfort of Knowing You're Not Alone
articles.description: Anxiety affects 40 million adults in the US. Here's what that community looks like.

articles.title: Cold Water and the Nervous System
articles.description: The science behind why cold water on your face can stop a panic attack in its tracks.

articles.title: Progressive Muscle Relaxation: The Research
articles.description: Decades of evidence behind one of anxiety's most underrated tools.

videos.title: Box Breathing with Andrew Huberman
videos.description: 4 minutes. A calm walkthrough of the technique from one of neuroscience's clearest communicators.

videos.title: A Guided Body Scan for Anxiety
videos.description: 10 minutes. Gentle, non-clinical, and easy to follow anywhere.

videos.title: Why Zebras Don't Get Ulcers
videos.description: Robert Sapolsky's legendary, funny lecture on stress biology. Perspective-shifting and genuinely entertaining.

videos.title: The Physiological Sigh Explained
videos.description: Andrew Huberman breaks down the double inhale exhale technique in under 3 minutes.

videos.title: Headspace: Anxiety Meditation
videos.description: A simple, approachable guided session from one of the most trusted names in meditation.

videos.title: How to Make Stress Your Friend
videos.description: Kelly McGonigal's TED Talk reframes stress in a way that genuinely helps. Warm, evidence-based, and a little life-changing.

videos.title: Funny Anxiety: You Are Not Your Thoughts
videos.description: A lighthearted animated short on what anxiety actually is -- and why your brain isn't broken.
